bull.b: Toast reversed earlier cash deficits and now produces durable free cash flow ( ~$608M in 2025 and ~$654M TTM). FCF tracks net income closely (~0.92), enabling reinvestment, opportunistic buybacks and cushioning cycles given the company's very low leverage and improving returns.

bull.t: High SaaS Margins And Recurring Growth
bull.b: Subscription and SaaS economics are high quality: SaaS gross margin >80%, subscription gross profit up materially and ARR growth near mid-20s. This durable, recurring mix supports scalable incremental margins, predictable revenue and profitable unit economics over the medium term.

A 171k-location base and continued net adds drive network effects for payments, SaaS uptake and cross-sell. Scale supports higher GPV ($51B) and monetization gains, making customer acquisition investments more leverageable and improving long-term competitive positioning.

bear.b: Revenue growth has materially slowed to ~4.8% TTM from prior double-digit years, elevating execution risk. Slower top-line expansion makes sustaining recent margin and profit improvements harder, raising sensitivity to churn, pricing pressure and competitive dynamics over the next several quarters.

Negative margins in hardware and pro services (≈ -13% of recurring gross profit) mechanically pull down consolidated profitability. Persistent tariff/input cost pressure and shipment economics can cap overall margin expansion despite strong SaaS performance, limiting operating leverage.

bear.t: Strategic Inventory Build Pressures Cash Conversion
bear.b: Management's intentional memory‑chip purchases and inventory build reduce adjusted EBITDA-to-FCF conversion, concentrating cash impact in Q2 and lowering near-term cash flexibility. Bigger P&L/inventory effects are anticipated into 2027, creating multi-quarter cash-conversion risk.
